subject: Computer Networking
title: TCP/IP: The Universal Language
description: One-minute skill: TCP/IP: The Universal Language.

hook.text: Different networks existed by the 1970s — but none could talk to each other. Vint Cerf and Bob Kahn fixed that.

buildup.text: They designed TCP/IP to work across any network type — wired, wireless, satellite. A universal protocol suite.

discovery.text: TCP handles reliable delivery. IP handles addressing and routing. Together they connect anything to anything.

twist.text: On January 1, 1983 — Flag Day — ARPANET switched from NCP to TCP/IP. Every host had to upgrade at once.

climax.text: That single switch unified networking forever. TCP/IP is still the foundation of the internet today.

punchline.text: TCP/IP didn't just connect networks. It became the internet.

quiz.question: When did ARPANET officially switch from NCP to TCP/IP?
